mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.5% signal aggressive monetary accommodation. This compression of the risk-free rate and reduction in discount rates applied to equity valuations produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.1% as of 2020-01-15 indicates ultra-loose monetary stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% do not exceed baseline returns by at least 50 basis points (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, signaling labor market deterioration and recession risk. This triggers equity risk-off positioning and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite unemployment rise signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) decelerates below 2% year-over-year while simultaneously housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1.0 million annualized units. This joint contraction in consumer spending and residential investment signals demand-side weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth < 2% AND HOUST < 1.0M units annualized
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 439891M and HOUST at 1001K as of 2020-01-15
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in triggered regimes exceed baseline by > 100 basis points, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) declines month-over-month for two consecutive months, indicating supply-side contraction and reduced corporate capacity utilization. This signals deteriorating business conditions and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as profit margins compress.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines MoM for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth accelerates above 200K new jobs per month while unemployment rate (UNRATE) simultaneously declines below 4.0%, indicating tight labor market conditions. This dual tightening produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as strong wage growth and consumer confidence support equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138780K and UNRATE at 6.1% as of 2020-01-15
regime_hint: tight labor market and late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in triggered regimes do not exceed baseline by at least 75 basis points, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 1.0%, creating negative real rates. This monetary accommodation in an inflationary environment produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity investors seek inflation hedges.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 237.08 and FEDFUNDS at 0.1% as of 2020-01-15
regime_hint: negative real rates with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in triggered regimes do not exceed baseline by at least 50 basis points, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate in the most recent quarter, signaling economic slowdown. This demand-side weakness produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as consensus earnings estimates are revised downward.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following GDP slowdown signals exceed baseline by > 100 basis points, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 1.3 million annualized units while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously grow above 4% year-over-year, indicating robust consumer demand and residential investment. This dual demand expansion produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as cyclical equity sectors outperform.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1.3M units annualized AND RSAFS YoY growth > 4%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1001K and RSAFS at 439891M as of 2020-01-15
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in triggered regimes do not exceed baseline by at least 25 basis points, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
